summary: Smooth linear overhead penalty replacing cliff formula, flagged as provisional
context: Original formula max(0, ratio - 0.10) * 200 had arbitrary 10% cliff and 200x multiplier with no empirical calibration. Disproportionately penalized Twining (15-25% overhead) while giving free pass to file-based approaches (5-10%).
rationale: Continuous linear formula (ratio * 100) gives proportional cost at all levels. Flagged as provisional pending calibration from first real benchmark runs. Formula field in metadata enables traceability.

alternatives.option: Keep cliff formula but calibrate threshold from Phase 0 data
alternatives.reason_rejected: No real data exists yet — smooth formula avoids distorting early results while we collect empirical evidence
Overhead is a real cost — removing it would unfairly favor high-overhead conditions
